instruction: Classify the shipment incident report into an incident category.
baseline_prompt: You are a logistics incident classifier. Classify the shipment incident report into an incident category. Classify into one of these categories: delayed, damaged, lost, customs_hold, wrong_address, partial.
source: Authored for TEI-Bench (Sonnet-generated, Sonnet-validated; kept only label-agreed examples).

train.query: The vessel departed the port two days behind schedule due to mechanical issues, which will impact our ability to meet the original delivery commitment to the warehouse.

Received complaint from consignee about wine shipment. 8 out of 24 bottles had broken seals and leaked contents throughout the container. Glass shards found mixed with remaining intact bottles. Temperature-controlled transport maintained proper conditions, but securing straps appear to have been too tight.

We have been unable to locate package tracking number XJ-88921 after it departed the Phoenix distribution center on March 15th. Multiple facility scans show no record of the parcel arriving at any subsequent sorting location, and the recipient has not received it despite 14 days passing since expected delivery.

Truck has been detained at the crossing point since Monday morning. Officials are conducting a physical inspection of the cargo and requesting translation of the packing list into the local language before authorization to enter the country.

Our driver confirms delivery to the street name provided, but the city was incorrect - package went to Springfield, MA when it should have gone to Springfield, IL. Customer never received their goods.

Customer called to report that their furniture set arrived with only the table and two chairs. The invoice clearly shows they ordered four chairs and a matching sideboard which were not included in the shipment.
Driver reported being stuck at a weigh station for over 5 hours due to inspection backlog, causing the delivery to miss the morning appointment slot.

Fresh produce shipment to restaurant chain shows extensive bruising and spoilage. Strawberries completely crushed in bottom layers, with visible mold growth. Packaging integrity maintained but stacking weight and vibration during transport caused mechanical injury to fruit.

Parcel with AWB number 4472839201 has vanished from our network. Final scan shows departure from Chicago O'Hare on 04/22, but the package never reached the local delivery station and exhaustive searches of all intermediate facilities have yielded nothing.

The pharmaceutical shipment cannot proceed past the international gateway as the regulatory agency requires import licenses and compliance documentation that were not included with the original submission. Estimated resolution time is 5-7 business days.
Delivery attempted to an incomplete location missing the suite number. Driver left package at the main office of the commercial building, but shipper had specified Suite 301. Item remains unclaimed.

Our receiving department signed for the delivery but after inspection discovered that several SKUs from the original order are completely absent from what was delivered. We still need items A-445, B-223, and C-889.
The connecting flight for our air freight was cancelled, and the next available departure isn't until tomorrow, putting us well past our service level agreement timeframe.

Furniture delivery resulted in refusal of acceptance. The leather sofa had a 6-inch tear along the backrest and multiple scuff marks on wooden legs. White glove service team documented the condition before customer sign-off. Appears to have occurred during warehouse handling based on timestamp.

The shipment containing 12 boxes of medical supplies cannot be accounted for. According to our system, the freight left the origin terminal but never checked in at the destination warehouse. Driver confirmed drop-off, but receiving dock has no record and surveillance footage shows no delivery occurred.

test.query: Import declaration was rejected due to incomplete information about the manufacturer. The clearance agent is working to obtain the necessary certificates and permits, but the merchandise will remain in the bonded warehouse until approval is granted.

Driver delivered the package to 123 Main Street instead of 123 Main Avenue as specified on the shipping label. Customer called stating they never received their order and confirmed their correct location is on the Avenue, not the Street.
Received the order today but only got 8 out of 12 boxes listed on the manifest. The remaining items are missing and we need them urgently to complete our inventory restocking.
Shipment is currently running 24 hours behind the original ETA because of unexpected road closures and detours around the construction zone on Highway 95.

Electronics order arrived with water infiltration. Customer opened packages to find moisture inside sealed boxes, corroded circuit boards, and non-functional units. Tracking shows shipment was exposed to heavy rainfall during transfer at Memphis hub where trailer door was reportedly left open.

Customer contacted us reporting their order never arrived. Investigation reveals the last scan was at our Memphis hub 3 weeks ago, with no further tracking updates. All warehouse searches have been conducted without success in recovering the item.

Container #MSCU4567812 remains at the port facility pending resolution of tariff classification dispute. The authorities are requesting a detailed commercial invoice with harmonized codes for the electronic components included in this consignment.

Recipient contacted us indicating the shipment went to their old residence from which they moved two months ago. The updated information was in our system but appears the label printed with outdated details.
The driver left three cartons at our warehouse dock, however the purchase order indicates we were expecting seven cartons total. Please investigate why the shipment is incomplete.
Weather conditions in the Midwest caused significant slowdowns on Route 80, and the shipment that was supposed to arrive Tuesday morning is now estimated for Thursday evening.

Upon delivery inspection, the customer reported that all 15 boxes of ceramic tiles had significant cracks and chips throughout. The external packaging showed clear signs of impact with torn cardboard and visible crushing on multiple sides. Driver confirmed rough handling during unloading.

Three pallets scheduled for delivery to the Portland distribution center are unaccounted for. The bill of lading was signed by our carrier, but the freight never materialized at the receiving dock. Cross-referencing with all regional facilities has produced no results regarding the current location of these goods.

Our freight forwarder reports that the package is being inspected by border protection services due to discrepancies in the declared value. They have requested proof of purchase and additional certificates of origin to validate the transaction.

Package scanned as delivered but recipient states it was brought to the neighbor's house three doors down. The GPS coordinates show the driver stopped at a location approximately 100 meters from the intended drop-off point.
The construction materials were delivered yesterday but we're short several pallets of cement bags and all the rebar that was ordered. Can you trace where the rest of our shipment ended up?
The truck arrived at the distribution center 3 hours past the expected delivery window due to heavy traffic on the interstate. Customer has been notified of the revised timeline.

Industrial machinery part arrived with bent hydraulic arm and scratched metal housing. Crate was intact with no external indicators of mishandling, but internal foam padding had shifted, allowing 200-pound component to move freely during the 3-day cross-country journey.

Recipient filed a claim stating the high-value electronics shipment was never delivered. Our records indicate the package was loaded onto delivery vehicle DV-2847 on Tuesday morning, but the driver has no recollection of the item and it's not present in the vehicle or warehouse upon inspection.
